locations.description: A small, rustic forest cabin belonging to a young orc huntress. The interior is simple, containing a stone fireplace with a stack of dry firewood, and various hunting tools including a bow and a quiver of arrows.

groups.description: A floor and log walls encompass the area, now illuminated by the warm, flickering glow of the fire in the central hearth. Maza is located in the center. A wooden door is set into the southern side. To the north, the window remains closed, and the central fireplace crackles with burning oak. The northeast section contains another fireplace and a stack of firewood logs. Hunting gear lines the western wall.

locations.description: A long 1x10 dirt road extending south from the hut's entrance.

groups.description: A Dirt Road extends across the entire length of the area. A Wooden Signpost is situated at the northern end of the path. A Cairn is placed further south along the road. An Animal Trap is located near the southern end of the path.

locations.description: A large, empty 10x10 meter clearing starting from the anchor point, extending away from the occupied zone.

groups.description: A Forest clearing occupies the entire space, serving as the base for the other objects. A Granite boulder is positioned in the center-left area, with a River pebble resting on top of it. A patch of Wild lavender is located in the northern section, while an Oak stump is situated in the southeastern part of the clearing.

history: Name: Maza. Race: Orc. Gender: Female. Height: 178cm. Weight: 82kg. Build: Athletic and lean. Distinctive Features: Pale green skin, yellow eyes, two small tusks protruding from her lower lip, and black hair tied in a high ponytail.
Moved to the western wall to access the quiver.
Retrieved the bundle of arrows (ID 17) from the quiver to use the flint heads.
Gathered dried moss from the walls to serve as tinder.
Prepared the fire lay in the fireplace.
Attempted to ignite the tinder by striking a flint arrowhead against the fireplace stones.
Successfully ignited the dried moss with sparks from the flint arrowhead. Blew gently on the embers until a small flame took hold.
Sat before the fire for a while, warming up and watching the flames dance.
A large, rustic fireplace built from heavy river stones. The hearth is currently cold and contains no lit embers.
The hearth is now active, radiating warmth and light.
A neat stack of dry, split oak firewood placed within the fireplace hearth.
Caught fire from the kindling, the dry oak logs beginning to burn steadily.
A bundle of twenty hunting arrows with sharp flint heads and grey goose feather fletching.

text.Text:
Maza crossed the worn wooden planks to the western wall, where her hunting gear hung in the shadows. She reached into her leather quiver and pulled out the bundle of arrows, seeking the sharp flint heads rather than the shafts themselves. With the ammunition in hand, she turned and stepped toward the central stone fireplace, which stood cold and silent in the morning light.

She needed tinder to wake the hearth. Scouring the log walls, she scraped a handful of dried moss from the crevices where it had been packed for insulation. She carried the fibrous clumps to the fireplace and arranged them carefully within the grate, positioning the dry oak firewood above the makeshift nest.

Holding an arrow steady, she struck the flint head against the rough river stones of the hearth. Sparks flew with a sharp clicking sound, landing amidst the moss. A faint wisp of smoke curled upward. Maza leaned close, blowing gently on the smoldering pile until a small tongue of flame erupted. The fire spread eagerly to the kindling and then to the oak logs, filling the cabin with the comforting crackle of burning wood and the scent of smoke. The hearth radiated a golden glow that illuminated the room, pushing back the gloom. Her task complete, Maza sat before the growing fire, letting the new warmth seep into her limbs as she watched the flames dance.

To the north, the window remains closed, and the central fireplace crackles with burning oak. The northeast section contains another fireplace and a stack of firewood logs.
